image_prompts.prompt: Hook visual: Ever wonder why pollen dances in water? It's not magic—it's atoms!
Context visual: This jittery movement is called Brownian motion, named after botanist Robert Brown.
Escalation visual: Brown noticed that pollen grains zigzagged due to tiny, invisible water molecules colliding with them.
Evidence visual: This was some of the first evidence for atoms, changing how we understand matter!
Payoff visual: Brownian motion proves that even the smallest particles can have a big impact!
Cta visual: Curious about more scientific wonders? Like and subscribe for more!
